title: Bogle's Folly
content:
August 1976. We launched the First Index Investment Trust.

Wall Street laughed. Called it 'Bogle's Folly.' Said it was 'un-American to settle for average.'

The IPO aimed for $150 million. We raised $11 million. The underwriters wanted to cancel.

I said no. 'We stay the course.'

Today, that fund holds over $500 billion. Index funds globally hold over $10 trillion.

Sometimes being laughed at is the price of being right.
